Facts.text: 15th recorded perihelion passage of Halley's Comet
Siege of Malbork, including its castle, seat of the Teutonic Order begun by Polish and Prussian forces - captured September 1454
Saxony and Hesse form League of Gotha (league of Protestant princes)
Evangelical German towns form Schmalkaldische Union
Osep Gregoriwich Napea, the 1st Russian ambassador to England, arrives in London
British composer William Byrd is appointed organist at Lincoln Cathedral, Lincoln, England
Amboyna Massacre: ten Englishmen and ten Japanese and a Portuguese are beheaded by the Dutch for supposedly planning to seize their fort on Ambon Island (modern Indonesia)
Yuan Chonghuan is appointed Governor of Liaodong, after he led the Chinese into a great victory against the Manchurians under Nurhaci
Battle at Elmina, Gold Coast: Dutch Vice-admiral De Ruyter captures Fort Cormantin from the English
Abraham Crijnssen conquers Fort Willoughby (Zeelandia), Suriname
Holy Roman Emperor Leopold I orders all Jews expelled from Austria, given until August to comply
Earl of Shaftesbury freed from the Tower of London
First women's magazine "The Ladies' Mercury" is published in London
English/Welsh nobles lay down Oath of Association
French troops bomb Willemstad, Curacao
Washington, D.C. placed under Congressional jurisdiction
Great fire in Bombay, India
First federal vaccination legislation enacted
US Congress authorizes use of steamboats to transport mail
First Mardi Gras celebration in New Orleans
Dominican Republic gains independence from Haiti (National Day)
Russians shoot at Poles protesting Russian rule of Poland in Castle Square, Warsaw
US Congress authorizes 1st stamped newspaper wrappers for mailing
6th and last day of battle at Dalton, Georgia (about 600 casualties)
Near Andersonville, Georgia, rebels open a new POW camp "Camp Sumpter"
Civil War skirmish near Sturgeon, Missouri
John Menard is 1st African American to make a speech in the US Congress
Meeting of Alabama claims commission
Charlotte Ray, 1st African American woman lawyer in USA, graduates from Howard University
Dutch socialist Samuel van Wooden demands law against child labor
Baseball 1st played in England at Lord's Cricket Ground
Russian-American chemist Constantin Fahlberg discovers an artificial sweetener (later named 'saccharin'), while working on coal tar analysis at Johns Hopkins University in Baltimore, Maryland
Battle at Amajuba: South African Boers vs British army under General Colley
Oscar Hammerstein patents 1st cigar-rolling machine
D Needham and P Kerrigan box 100 rounds (6 h 39 m) in San Francisco; match is a draw
Battle of Paardeberg: Boer General Piet Cronjé surrenders to the British after a prolonged siege
The Trades Union Congress and the Independent Labour Party (formed in 1893) meet in London, resulting in a Labour Representative Committee and eventually the modern UK Labour Party in 1906

A General Committee of National Liberal Federation meets and adopts a resolution deploring the continuation of the war in South Africa and condemning the British Government's insistence on unconditional surrender by the Boers
National League Rules Committee for baseball decrees that all fouls are to count as strikes except after two strikes
France and Britain agree to joint control of New Hebrides
Sacrifice fly adopted (repealed in 1931, reinstated 1954)
1st public performance of Holst's "Planets"
American Association for Hard of Hearing forms (NYC)
The Fascists incite a riot in Florence, Italy
The International Working Union of Socialist Parties is founded in Vienna
G B Shaw's "Back to Methusaleh I/II" premieres in NYC
US Supreme Court unanimously upholds 19th amendment to the US Constuituent - women's right to vote
Belgium's Theunis government falls
Turkey signs Litvinov-pact
Bouvet Island declared a Norwegian dependency
Explosion in coal mine Boissevain, Virginia, USA leaves 38 dead
Dutch swimmer Willy den Ouden sets new women's world 100m freestyle record (1:04.6) in Amsterdam; lasts 22 years until broken in 1956 by Dawn Fraser of Australia
Belgian government of Pierlot falls
Borley Rectory, "the most haunted house in England", destroyed in a fire
Supreme Court outlaws sit-down strikes
Martin Kamen and Sam Ruben discover carbon-14 (radiocarbon dating) at the University of California Radiation Laboratory in Berkeley, California
1st transport of French Jews to nazi-Germany
Battle of Java Sea began: 13 US warships sunk and 2 Japanese
Radio emissions from the Sun first detected by English physicist and radio astronomer James Stanley Hey, working on WWII radar anti-jamming  methods

The Rosenstrasse Protest, a rare mass public demonstration in Germany against the Third Reich's deportation of Jews in Berlin; the protest by non-Jewish wives and relatives of detained Jewish men lasts a week and results in the release of approximately 1,800 captives
The Smith Mine No. 3 in Bearcreek, Montana, explodes, killing 74 men
University of Munich professor Kurt Huber, advisor to student non-violent resistance group "White Rose", arrested by Nazis
Battle of US 94 Infantry
4th "Road" film "Road to Utopia" premieres (NYC)
French explorer Paul-Emile Victor founds French Polar Expeditions to oversee French scientific missions
22nd amendment ratified, limiting US Presidents to 2 terms
Female suffrage granted in Egypt
Premiere of only prime-time network TV show beginning with an "X": "Xavier Cugat Show" on NBC (until X-Files)
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
Oil pipe line from Rotterdam to Ruhrgebied opens

Veikko Hakulinen of Finland wins his 3rd career Olympic cross country gold medal as part of Finland's 4 x 10k relay team at Squaw Valley, CA; winner: 50k (Oslo, 1952) and 30k (Cortina d’Ampezzo, 1956)
The first congress of the Spanish Trade Union Organisation is inaugurated
The government of Italy asks for help to keep the Leaning Tower of Pisa from toppling over.
Dutch Marijnen government resigns
France performs Underground nuclear test at Ecker Algeria
Ice Dance Championship at Davos won by Diane Towler and Bernard Ford of great Britain
Ice Pairs Championship at Davos won by Belousova and Protopopov of the Soviet Union
Antigua & St Christopher-Nevis become associated states of UK
Dominica gains independence from England
Rio de la Plata Treaty
NY Times (falsely) reports US army has ended domestic surveillance
Doctors in the first Dutch abortion clinic (the Mildredhuis in Arnhem) start to perform aborti provocati
American Indian Movement occupy Wounded Knee in South Dakota
MLB Chicago White Sox slugger Dick Allen signs 3 year $750,000 contract
"People" magazine begins sales
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
CDU-politician Peter Lorentz kidnapped in West Berlin
US House of Representatives pass $21.3 billion anti-recession tax-cut bill
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Israel & Egypt exchange ambassadors
Terrorists occupies Dominican embassy in Bogota
Greatest passenger load on a commercial airliner-610 on Boeing 747
Dan Issel (NBA-Nuggets), hits on 63rd consecutive free throw
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Wayne Williams found guilty of murdering 2 of 28 blacks in Atlanta
Eamonn Coghlan of Ireland set indoor mile record of 3:49.78
Worker's union leader Billy Nair (55) freed from Robben Island Prison in South Africa after 20 years as a political prisoner
WRC-AM in Washington, D.C. changes call letters to WWRC
14,000 American farmers converge in Ames, Iowa to demand economic relief
Mauritania's new constitutional charter published
US dollar is worth Ÿ3.9355 (Netherlands)
The United States Senate allows its debates to be televised on a trial basis
"Washington Week In Review" 20th anniversary on PBS
American Mike Conley sets world indoor triple jump record (17.76m) in New York
Donald Regan resigns as White House chief of staff
NCAA cancels SMU's entire 1987 football schedule for gross violations of NCAA rules regarding athletic corruption
Gulfstream G-IV goes around the world 36:08:34
Katarina Witt (GDR) wins 2nd consecutive Olympic figure skating
German war criminals Aus der Funten and Fischer freed in Holland
Venezuela is rocked by the riots of Caracazo
"Hold On" single released by Wilson Phillips (Billboard Song of the Year 1990)
Exxon Corp and Exxon Shipping are indicted on 5 criminal counts (Valdez)
Ben Elton's "Silly Cow" premieres in London
Noureddine Morcelli set 1500m mark at 3:34:16
Larry Smith named 9th Commissioner of the CFL
PBA National Championship Won by Ron Palombi Jr
Maronite church near Beirut bombed, 10 killed
XVII Winter Olympic Games close in Lillehammer, Norway
Car bomb explodes in Zakho, North-Iraq (54-80 killed)
First appearance of Pokémon in the role-playing video game "Pocket Monsters Red and Green" for Game Boy in Japan
Mark Waugh scores 126 in World Cup against India
"Last Night of Ballyhoo" opens at Helen Hayes Theater NYC
British singer Sade arrested in Jamaica for disobeying a police officer
Apple discontinues development of the Newton computer
Britain's House of Lords agrees to end 1,000 years of male precedence by giving a monarch's first-born daughter the same claim to the throne as any first born son
FBI arrests 10 most wanted suspected serial killer Tony Ray Amati
NE Patriot David Meggett arrested in Toronto on sex assault charges
Aston Villa are the last Premier League team to play a match with an all-English starting XI in a 4-1 defeat by Coventry
Korea International School is founded by Soon-Il Chung
Olusegun Obasanjo becomes Nigeria's first elected president since mid-1983
Godhra train burning, a Muslim mob kills 59 Hindu pilgrims returning from Ayodhya
Ryanair Flight 296 catches fire in London Stansted Airport. Subsequent investigations criticize Ryanair's handling of the evacuation.
Former Bosnian Serb leader Biljana Plavsic is sentenced by the U.N. tribunal in The Hague, Netherlands, to 11 years in prison
Rowan Williams is enthroned as the 104th Archbishop of Canterbury.
A bombing of a Superferry by Abu Sayyaf in the Philippines worst terrorist attack kills 116
Former BPMC general secretary Ordrick Samuel launches a new party in Barbuda, Barbudans for a Better Barbuda
The Chinese Correction: the Shanghai Stock Exchange falls 9%, the largest drop in 10 years
The general strike against Lansana Conté in Guinea ends
Statistics Finland informs that Finland's gross domestic product diminished by 1.3% in the last quarter of 2008 from the previous quarter
8.8 magnitude earthquake rattles central Chile; epicenter just off the coast also triggers a tsunami
17 Afghan militia are killed by Taliban insurgents in an attack in the Andar District
20 people are killed in a market fire in Calcutta, India
Arseniy Yatsenyuk appointed Prime Minister of Ukraine
Chaos erupts after the Swedish Public Employment Service mistakenly invites 61,000 people to a job interview in Stockholm
Republic of Crimea announces a referendum and ousts its regional government
Unmarked Russia special forces invade neighboring Crimea, Ukraine to assist pro-Russian nationalists, occupying government buildings in preparation for annexation
US Republican Governor Jan Brewer vetoes a "religious freedom" bill that would have allowed businesses to turn away gay customers
Russian politician Boris Nemtsov is assassinated in Moscow
Doctors announce world's second known case of semi-identical twins, a boy and girl from Brisbane, Australia
First gun control legislation for 25 years passed by US House of Representatives, with new federal background checks
Pakistan shoots down an Indian fighter jet and captures the pilot, further escalating tensions in the disputed Kashmir region
Smallest baby boy ever born to be later successfully released, goes home, after original birth weight of 268g (9.45oz) from Tokyo hospital
Train crashes into a buffer at Cairo's main railway station killing 25 and injuring dozens in Egypt
Biggest cosmic explosion ever detected from a supermassive black hole in Ophiuchus galaxy 390 million lights years away, (size of 15 Miky Ways), published in "Astrophysical Journal"
Dow Jones Industrial Average suffers its biggest points fall in history closing down 1,190.95 in New York amid concerns about COVID-19
US authorizes its third COVID-19 vaccine from Johnson & Johnson, a one-shot vaccine
Countries sanction Russia for its invasion of Ukraine; EU closes its airspace to Russian planes, Russian banks excluded from worldwide Swift payment system, Sweden sends arms to Ukraine
EU warns Russian invasion of Ukraine means Europe facing major humanitarian crisis, 18 million Ukrainians displaced with four million refugees fleeing the country
First confirming sighting of a sexual encounter between humpback whales, and the first homosexual coupling, published in "Marine Mammal Science"
Quickly spreading wildfires across Texas Panhandle propel Governor Greg Abbott to issue a disaster declaration for 60 counties
Henry IVcrowned King of France at Chartres Cathedral, only French monarch to be crowned there (Reins and Paris occupied by the Catholic League)
English explorerWilliam Dampieris the first British person to visit the Pacific Island of New Britain, which he names
The flag of Argentina, created byManuel Belgrano, is first raised at Rosario
Ludwig van Beethoven's Symphony No. 8 in F major premieres in Vienna
Dutch regain Suriname from the French after the defeat ofNapoleon
German composerRobert Schumannsaved from suicide attempt in Rhine River
Abraham Lincolnmakes a speech at Cooper Union in the city of New York that is largely responsible for his election to the Presidency
PsychiatristsCarl JungandSigmund Freudmeet for the first time in Vienna
Lord Kitcheneropens Khartoum-El Obeid (Nyala) railway
George Bernard Shaw's largest-scale play "Back to Methusaleh" premieres at Garrick Theatre in NYC
US Commerce SecretaryHerbert Hooverconvenes 1st National Radio Conference
Adolf Hitlerresurrects NSDAP political party in Munich
Test Cricket debut ofClarrie Grimmett, who took 5-45 & 6-37 v England
Jean Genet's play "Intermezzo" premieres in Paris
7th Academy Awards: "It Happened One Night",Clark Gable, andClaudette Colbertwin
Australian cricketerDon Bradmanscores 169 in the 5th Test match against England in 223 minutes, ensuring Australia wins the Ashes 3-2
Britain and France recognizeFrancisco Franco's government in Spain
13th Academy Awards: "Rebecca",James Stewart&Ginger Rogerswin
Chaim Weizmannbecomes 1st President of Israel
Mao Zedong's famous speech to the Supreme State Conference "On Correct Handling of Contradictions Among People" expounding Maoist ideals
Boston CelticBob Cousysets NBA record with 28 assists Boston Celtics score 173 points against Minneapolis Lakers
US Olympic Ice Hockey Team beats USSR 3-2 en route to gold medal
South Vietnam PresidentNgô Đình Diệm's palace bombed by dissident air pilots in a failed assassination attempt
Mickey Mantleof the NY Yankees signs a baseball contract worth $100,000
8th Daytona 500: PolesitterRichard Pettycomes from 2 laps down to win the event after 198 laps were completed because of rain
CBS News anchorWalter Cronkitedelivers a scathing editorial on America's chances of winning the Vietnam War
GeneralHafez al-Assadbecomes head of Syria via military coup
US PresidentRichard Nixonvisits West Berlin, West Germany
US PresidentRichard Nixonand Chinese PremierZhou Enlaiissue Shanghai Communique
PopePaul VIpublishes constitution motu proprio Quo aptius
Rolling Stones' guitaristKeith Richardsgets suspended sentence for heroin possession in Canada
Swedish pop groupABBAarrives in Australia for the first time, sparking "ABBA-mania"
22nd Grammy Awards: Song of the Year - Kenny Loggins' "What A Fool Believes"; Best Album -Billy Joel's "52nd Street"
Paul McCartneyand Stevie Wonder record the single "Ebony & Ivory"
Earl Anthonybecomes 1st pro bowler to win more than $1 million
Carl Lewisjumps world record indoor (8.675 m)
Bonnie Blair(US) wins Olympic 500m speed skating in record 39.1
SingerJames Brownis paroled from prison after serving 2 years of 6 year sentence for weapon and drug related convictions
American film production company "Happy Madison Productions" is founded byAdam Sandler
44th Grammy Awards: "Walk On";Alicia Keyswin
Singer-songwriterJoni Mitchellreceives Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award
77th Academy Awards: "Million Dollar Baby",Jamie Foxx, andHilary Swankwin
Mark Messier's #11 jersey is retired by the Edmonton Oilers
83rd Academy Awards: Best Picture "The King's Speech",Colin FirthandNatalie Portmanwin best acting awards
83rd Academy Awards:Trent Reznorand Atticus Ross win Best Original Score for 'The Social Network'
WikiLeaksbegins disclosing five million emails from the private intelligence company Stratfor
PopeBenedict XVIpresents his farewell address to Vatican City
70's glam rockerGary Glitter(real name Paul Gadd) sentenced to 16 years in prison for various child sex convictions
Barbra Streisandreveals she has cloned her dog twice
Jared Kushner, son-in-law of President Trump, has his top-secret security clearance at the White House downgraded
Laureus World Sports Awards, Monte-Carlo Sporting, Monaco: Sportsman:Roger Federer; Sportswoman:Serena Williams; Team: Mercedes F1 team
"Captain Marvel" film directed by Anna Boden and Ryan Fleck, starringBrie LarsonandSamuel L. Jacksonpremieres
ActorLuke Perry(52) suffers a stroke in Los Angeles
Michael Cohen, former lawyer toDonald Trumptestifies before the House Oversight Committee saying Trump is "racist", a "con man" and "a cheat"
Second summit begins between US PresidentDonald Trumpand North Korea's leaderKim Jong-unin Hanoi, Vietnam
New post-Brexit deal for Northern Ireland announced by UK Prime MinisterRishi Sunakand President of the European CommissionUrsula von der Leyen
The Philadelphia Orchestra announces renaming of their concert home asMarian AndersonHall, in honor of the noted 20th century contralto who was from the city
Nazi Germany's parliament building, the Reichstag, is destroyed by fire, possibly set by the Nazis, who blame and execute Dutch communistMarinus van der Lubbe
Final day of the rum ration in the Royal New Zealand Navy
Franciscus Raphelengius, Flemish book publisher (1st Arabic-Latin dictionary), born in Lannoy (d. 1587)
Carel Fabritius, Dutch painter (pupil of Rembrandt), born in Middenbeemster, Dutch Republic (d. 1654)
Johann Philipp Krieger, German Baroque organist and composer, baptized in the Free Imperial City of Nuremberg, Holy Roman Empire (d. 1725)
Pietro Gnocchi, Italian sacred music composer, choir director Brescia Cathedral, 1723-33 and 1762-75), priest, historian, and geographer, born in Alfianello, Brescia, Republic of Venice (d. 1775).
Edward Cave, English editor and publisher (Gentlemen's Magazine, coined term "magazine"), born in Newton, England (d. 1754)
Johann Valentin Gorner, German composer, born in Penig, Electorate of Saxony, Holy Roman Empire (d. 1762)

Baldomero Espartero, Spanish general, statesman and Regent of Spain for Isabella II (1840-43), born in Granátula de Calatrav, Spain (d. 1879)

Richard Garnett, English librarian and writer (Keeper of Printed Books British Museum), born in Lichfield, England (d. 1906)
[Eleanor] Agnes Lee, American daughter of US Confederate general Robert E. Lee, born in at Arlington, Virginia (d. 1873)
Joaquin Valverde Durán Spanish composer, born in Badajoz, Spain (d. 1910)
Ellen Alice Terry, British Shakespearean actress and director (Imperial Theatre), born in Coventry, Warwickshire, England (d. 1928)
Hubert Parry, English musicologist and composer (Jerusalem), born in Bournemouth, England (d. 1918)
Henry Edwards Huntington, American railroad executive (owned Pacific Electric Railway), born in Oneonta, New York (d. 1927)
Alfred Einhorn, German chemist (created Novocain), born in Hamburg, Germany (d. 1917)[1]
Adelaide Hoodless, Canadian educational reformer (founder of the Women's Institute), born in St George, Canada West (d. 1910)[1]
Rudolph Steiner, Austrian-born spiritualist (founder of doctrine of anthroposophy), born in Kraljevic, Austria (d. 1925)
Anastasios Metaxas, Greek architect (restored Panathinaiko Stadium 1896 Olympics Athens) and shooter (Olympic bronze trap, single shot 1908), born in Athens, Greece (d. 1937)
Irving Fisher ' America's greatest economist' (Fisher’s Theory of Interest, Monetarism), born in Saugerties, New York (d. 1947)[1]

Renaat Verheijen, Flemish actor and director (Innocent Heart), born in Belgium (d. 1930)

José Melis, Cuban-American pianist, orchestra leader, and television personality (Jack Paar Program), born in Havana, Cuba (d. 2005)

Dexter Gordon, American jazz tenor saxophonist (Go!; The Other Side of 'Round Midnight), and actor ('Round Midnight), born in Los Angeles, California (d. 1990)
Peter Fowler, British physicist who studied cosmic rays and radiation, born in England (d. 1996)
